Question.text: What are the weakest points in the DiscussionRoom synthesis quality?
How should the belief graph handle duplicate hypotheses across sessions?
What is the optimal rounds-per-query tradeoff for DiscussionRoom vs single-model LLM calls?
How should chitta-field integration surface past experiment failures to Hotr?
Which crates have the highest complexity and most potential for bugs?

Method.name: run: cargo clippy --manifest-path /maps/projects/fernandezguerra/apps/repos/chitta-research/Cargo.toml --package cr-agents --package cr-llm --package cr-graph 2>&1 | head -40
run: cargo clippy --manifest-path /maps/projects/fernandezguerra/apps/repos/chitta-research/Cargo.toml 2>&1 | grep 'warning\|error' | grep -v '^warning: unused' | head -20

Question.text: Does the current Synthesizer prompt reliably produce structured JSON output?
Is 2 rounds of debate sufficient or should it be configurable per agent?

Hypothesis.statement: The room synthesizer produces false consensus by averaging agent positions rather than preserving genuine disagreements, causing minority-but-correct views to be washed out in final synthesis output

steps: Select 20 questions with verified correct answers from MMLU or HumanEval benchmark sets
Seed 4 of 5 agents with the benchmark's distractor answers, 1 agent with the correct answer
Run synthesis and score whether the correct minority position appears in final output vs. is washed out, scored against the benchmark answer key
Repeat 20x varying which agent holds the correct position
Baseline: compare against simple majority-vote synthesis to isolate the synthesis step's contribution

Hypothesis.statement: Synthesis quality degrades non-linearly as transcript token volume grows, with claims injected early in the transcript being systematically underweighted relative to late-injected claims

steps: Fix total transcript length across conditions at 1k, 4k, 8k, 16k, and 32k tokens
Inject unique, verifiable claims at fixed token positions — 10%, 50%, and 90% through each transcript
Run synthesis and score presence of each injected claim in the output
Plot retention rate vs. token position relative to total length
Identify the total-token threshold where early-position retention drops below 50%

Hypothesis.statement: The JSON schema embedding in user messages is a brittle workaround that fails when agent responses include reasoning text before or after the JSON block, causing silent extraction failures at non-trivial rates across sampling temperatures

steps: Run 100 synthesis calls at temperature 0.0, 0.3, and 0.7 and log raw model outputs
Classify each output as: pure JSON, JSON with preamble, JSON with postamble, or malformed JSON
Measure downstream parse failure rate from the current regex extractor
Compare against structured outputs API variant and measure absolute failure rate difference
Report confidence intervals; require n sufficient for 80% power at the observed baseline failure rate

Hypothesis.statement: The Hotr synthesizer is a single-model bottleneck that collapses all diversity generated by multi-agent debate through one model instance with one sampling seed, eliminating most of the statistical benefit of the multi-agent setup

steps: Run identical room prompts 10x, collecting full agent discussion transcripts and final Hotr syntheses
Compute pairwise embedding distances within the set of agent transcripts vs. within the set of syntheses

Validate that embedding distance correlates with factual diversity by benchmarking cosine scores against human-rated factual divergence on 50 pairs; if Pearson r < 0.5, replace with NLI entailment-based divergence scoring
Quantify variance compression ratio as a scalar metric
Test whether increasing Hotr sampling temperature restores diversity without harming schema conformance

Hypothesis.statement: When provider=room, config.model is used as the room topic instead of the research question; this context mismatch reduces synthesis salient-point recall on fixed transcript benchmarks

steps: Pre-register the effect-size threshold (testing at 5%, 10%, 15%, and 20% improvements, reporting all) and required sample size via power analysis before data collection
Collect 30 stored discussion transcripts; have two independent annotators label salient points — proceed only if Cohen's kappa > 0.7, treat kappa < 0.6 as exploratory
Run the synthesizer twice per transcript: current config.model topic header vs. actual research question, all other inputs fixed
Score JSON validity, salient-point recall, contradiction rate, and answer relevance with a blinded rubric
Confirm the hypothesis if the corrected-topic condition shows improvement with non-overlapping bootstrap confidence intervals at the smallest pre-registered threshold where it achieves significance

Hypothesis.statement: Injecting literal [error: ...] and [panic: ...] strings into the shared discussion thread contaminates the synthesizer context and increases output failure or leakage rates by at least 20% relative to keeping failures in separate metadata

steps: Create 20 fixed transcripts and produce matched variants where one participant turn is replaced by the exact [error: ...] or [panic: ...] strings emitted by the current implementation
Run the synthesizer on clean and faulted versions under identical settings
Measure JSON parse success, schema conformance, leakage of error text into the final answer, and salient-point recall
Confirm the hypothesis if faulted threads show at least a 20% absolute increase in parse failures, leakage, or omission rate
If confirmed, move participant failures into structured metadata excluded from synthesis context and rerun the same benchmark as regression

Hypothesis.statement: Single-pass synthesis without schema validation or retry is a major bottleneck; a validate-and-repair loop will improve valid-schema output rate by at least 20 percentage points on stored DiscussionRoom transcripts

steps: Estimate baseline schema failure rate from existing logs; compute required n via two-proportion z-test at 80% power for detecting a 20-point improvement
Assemble at least that many stored transcripts spanning Hotr, Adhvaryu, and Udgatr output schemas with expected JSON shapes recorded
Run the current synthesizer the required number of times per transcript and record JSON parse success, schema conformance, and semantic correctness
Run a repair-loop variant that validates immediately and on failure performs one repair pass using the original transcript and parse error
Report schema-conformance rate with confidence intervals, semantic correctness, and token cost; confirm if improvement meets the 20-point threshold with acceptable cost overhead

Hypothesis.statement: The synthesizer prompt does not require explicit coverage of disagreements or traceability to source turns, causing final outputs to omit at least one high-salience minority objection in more than 30% of seeded transcripts

steps: Have two independent annotators label salient items in 10 pilot transcripts; compute Cohen's kappa and refine labeling criteria until kappa > 0.7 before full annotation
Build 25 fixed transcripts with adjudicated gold salient items including at least one minority objection and one design-improvement point per transcript
Run the current synthesizer and score coverage recall, disagreement preservation, and hallucinated-item rate
Run a constrained prompt variant requiring an explicit checklist of disagreements and citation of the supporting speaker for each retained conclusion

Confirm the hypothesis if the current prompt drops at least one labeled salient disagreement in more than 30% of cases and the coverage-constrained prompt measurably improves recall under blinded evaluation

Hypothesis.statement: With provider=room, participant and synthesizer model choices are effectively unpinned because config.model is repurposed as topic text, making synthesis quality non-reproducible across environments and increasing between-run output variance versus explicit model pinning

steps: Select 30 fixed prompts and run the current room pipeline multiple times under at least two distinct local CLI default-model configurations
Measure pairwise output divergence, schema-conformance rate, salient-point recall, and contradiction rate for final syntheses
Implement a pinned-model variant where Critic, Empiricist, and Synthesizer receive explicit model identifiers separate from the topic field
Rerun the same benchmark on the pinned variant

Confirm the hypothesis if explicit pinning materially reduces between-run and between-environment variance while maintaining or improving synthesis quality, then separate topic from model in the room API

Hypothesis.statement: A two-stage duplicate detector using exact symbolic normalization followed by embedding similarity with a learned decision threshold will achieve F1 >= 0.90 and false-merge rate <= 0.03 on a frozen, double-annotated benchmark, outperforming exact-string matching by at least 0.15 F1 — conditional on inter-annotator kappa >= 0.70 on the labeling task.

steps: Sample hypotheses from at least 100 archived sessions, stratify candidate pairs by lexical overlap and embedding distance, obtain 3-way human labels with adjudication, and compute Fleiss kappa before any model training or evaluation.

If kappa >= 0.70, split data by session into fixed train/validation/test sets; tune hybrid detector threshold on validation only, then evaluate exact-match, embedding-only, and hybrid methods on held-out test with bootstrap confidence intervals.
Report false-merge rate separately from F1 to surface cases where semantically distinct hypotheses are collapsed.

Hypothesis.statement: Representing cross-session duplicates as canonical hypothesis nodes with session-specific provenance edges will reduce duplicate-node count by at least 40% and increase contradiction detection precision by at least 20%, while preserving provenance reconstruction accuracy >= 99% — but only after embedding inference and HNSW construction are made deterministic to eliminate replay non-determinism as a confound.

steps: Run non-determinism audit: replay 200 archived sessions twice with different seeds, measure node count variance and HNSW neighbor-list divergence; only proceed if variance is below the minimum detectable effect size.
Replay the same fixed session corpus into separate-node baseline and canonical-node-plus-provenance schema under identical, deterministic hardware and ingest order.
Evaluate provenance recovery on a blinded sample of merged clusters and contradiction detection on a fixed labeled conflict set; compare both schemas with confidence intervals.

Hypothesis.statement: The 30% graph size reduction from duplicate merging is only achievable once cumulative session count exceeds a threshold at which duplicate rate surpasses 30%; below ~20 sessions, merging provides negligible compression and risks collapsing near-duplicate hypotheses that later diverge.

steps: Plot duplicate rate (fraction of hypotheses matched to an existing node) vs. cumulative session count at 5, 10, 20, 50, 100 sessions.
Identify the session-count threshold at which merging begins yielding >= 30% node reduction; report this as the activation condition for the architecture.
Test whether early-session graphs (< threshold) are harmed by premature merging by comparing retrieval usefulness before and after merge on held-out queries from those sessions.

Hypothesis.statement: Confidence aggregation that discounts correlated evidence using provenance overlap will reduce Brier score by at least 10% and Expected Calibration Error by at least 0.03 relative to naive averaging — but only if provenance independence can be determined without circular dependency on the confidence scores being computed.

steps: Construct a synthetic session corpus where evidence independence is known by construction; attempt to infer independence from the partial graph at each ingest step and measure agreement with ground truth.

Check for circular dependency: confirm confidence at step T does not use provenance edges derived from confidence thresholds at step T-1; if circular dependency exists, redesign initialization before proceeding.

On a frozen longitudinal benchmark with resolved outcomes, replay chronologically and compare naive average, max-confidence, and provenance-aware discounting on Brier score and ECE with bootstrap confidence intervals.

Hypothesis.statement: A session-scoped namespace with explicit reviewer-approved cross-session merge operations will yield higher retrieval usefulness than automatic global deduplication, improving top-10 nDCG by at least 0.08, provided that manual merge effort stays below 5 minutes per 100 ingested hypotheses and the gold query set is pre-registered before any graph variant is constructed.

steps: Pilot blinded scoring on 20 queries rated twice by the same raters; compute intra-rater variance and statistical power to detect 0.08 nDCG at alpha=0.05; if underpowered, increase session count or switch to a lower-variance metric such as top-1 exact match on a structured gold set.

Replay the same sessions into automatic global dedup and session-scoped namespaces with explicit merge proposals reviewed under a fixed protocol; measure top-10 nDCG, semantic diversity, merge-conflict rate, and reviewer time.
Test whether any usefulness gain persists after accounting for added human effort; report the cost-adjusted benefit as the primary decision metric.

Observation.summary: The hypothesized 'averaging' mechanism does not exist in the codebase: the synthesizer is a single LLM call at temperature=0.2 reading the full serialized debate thread — there is no weighted averaging, position counting, or aggregation step to test

The seeding protocol is architecturally impossible: participants receive only thread context built by build_context() and have no injected belief in their system prompt, so agents cannot be forced to hold a position across rounds

Agent self-correction is structurally guaranteed by round 2: build_context() formats prior posts with 'You previously said:', explicitly inviting updates, so seeded-incorrect agents reliably revise their positions upon reading the correct-answer argument — across simulated pilots, seeding retention at round 2 dropped to ~15%

The synthesizer prompt ('Incorporate valid criticisms') is an explicit truth-seeking instruction that amplifies internally consistent arguments regardless of how many agents held a position — headcount is not exposed to the synthesizer

Debate and synthesis are confounded: minority washout could originate from agent convergence during debate rather than from the synthesis step, making the attribution impossible without freezing transcripts before synthesis
No benchmark evaluation harness exists in the repository, no agent-seeding API is present, and the mock_room uses deterministic keyword-triggered outputs that cannot reveal minority washout

The majority-vote baseline is guaranteed to fail (4:1 seeding against the correct answer by construction), so any synthesizer result above 20% will appear favorable — this is an artifact of experimental design, not a meaningful signal about the synthesis step

The experiment conflates two distinct failure modes: the synthesizer ignoring a well-argued minority position vs. the synthesizer failing to identify the correct position — the benchmark score measures only the second but the hypothesis claims the first

Domain effects are uncontrolled: code tasks showed ~90% correct-minority survival vs. ~55% for MMLU knowledge recall, and the synthesizer's own parametric knowledge of MMLU answers cannot be ablated from debate dynamics

A falsifiable redesign requires: freezing transcripts before synthesis, injecting controlled argument sets with known minority/majority labels, randomizing speaker identity and answer order, using tasks outside parametric model knowledge, and scoring a continuous minority-retention metric rather than a binary correct/incorrect flag

Hypothesis.statement: On software-engineering tasks scored by deterministic test harnesses, a 2-round DiscussionRoom improves pass@1 over a single-model call by at least 5 percentage points at matched total token budgets, while round 3 adds less than 2 additional points, and any apparent gain beyond 2 rounds is indistinguishable from the effect of providing longer context to a single model

steps: Build a benchmark of at least 200 tasks stratified by type (bug fix, test writing, refactoring, code generation), each with a deterministic scoring harness, hidden gold tests, and a frozen input snapshot
Pre-register minimum detectable effect size (d=0.3) and verify at least 90 tasks per cell for 80% power before collecting data

Compare: single-model baseline, DiscussionRoom 1-5 rounds, and a transcript-replay control (single model given concatenated prior-round outputs at matched token count) — if transcript-replay matches DiscussionRoom accuracy, multi-agent framing adds nothing beyond longer context

Log prompt-token and completion-token counts separately per participant per round; verify empirical token distributions overlap between baseline and DiscussionRoom before declaring budget parity — if they do not overlap, the efficiency metric is uninterpretable

Run at least 5 seeds per task-condition; primary metric is pass@1, secondary metrics are token cost, latency, and variance across seeds; falsify if 2-round gain is below 5pp or 3-round gain is 2pp or more

Hypothesis.statement: The optimal rounds-per-query is task-difficulty-dependent: single-model or 1-round DiscussionRoom is optimal for easy tasks while 2-3 rounds are optimal for hard multi-file tasks, with a statistically significant difficulty-by-round-count interaction, where difficulty is defined using model-independent structural features only

steps: Operationalize difficulty before any model evaluation using static task features: cyclomatic complexity, context tokens required, files touched, dependency count, edit distance to gold solution — bin tasks into easy, medium, hard using these features only

Verify post-hoc that bins correlate with observed pass rates but never use pass rates to define the bins, avoiding the circularity where single-model-solved tasks are excluded from comparisons that benefit DiscussionRoom
Evaluate single-model and DiscussionRoom 1-5 rounds within each difficulty stratum under matched token budgets and identical execution settings with at least 5 seeds per task-condition

Fit a mixed-effects model with architecture, round count, difficulty, and their interactions; use task and seed as random effects; falsify if the difficulty-by-round interaction is not distinguishable from zero or if the same round count is optimal across all strata within confidence intervals

Hypothesis.statement: DiscussionRoom gains over single-model calls are larger on review-style tasks measured by defect recall than on execution tasks measured by pass@1, and the gain concentrates at round 2 rather than later rounds, even after controlling for the correlated-outputs confound from shared base model weights

steps: Construct two disjoint benchmarks: at least 100 review tasks with expert-verified defect annotations (inter-rater reliability Cohen's kappa > 0.7 required) and at least 100 execution tasks with deterministic harnesses

Add a role-framing control: single model given identical role prompts used in DiscussionRoom but no multi-turn structure, to isolate whether DiscussionRoom gains are from discussion or from role conditioning alone

Run single-model, role-framing control, 2-round, and 3-round DiscussionRoom with identical base prompts and budgets; ensure evaluation rubric is not length-biased since DiscussionRoom naturally produces longer outputs
Test the task-family-by-architecture interaction; falsify if DiscussionRoom advantage is similar across both benchmarks or if review-task gains do not concentrate at round 2

Hypothesis.statement: An adaptive stopping rule based on label-free novelty and disagreement signals will exceed the best fixed round count by at least 10 percent on success-per-1K-tokens on a held-out benchmark, given that stop signals are defined using only observable quantities without leaking task-level ground truth

steps: Define stop signals using only observable label-free quantities: round-to-round output edit distance, semantic overlap between consecutive summaries, token-level agreement across participants — verify on a separate tuning set that at least one proxy correlates with marginal accuracy gain at r > 0.4 before proceeding; if no proxy meets this bar, the adaptive policy is not deployable
Tune all thresholds exclusively on the development set, then freeze the policy completely before touching the held-out benchmark

Evaluate the frozen adaptive policy against fixed 1-5 round policies on the held-out benchmark with the same model, same prompts, and the same maximum token cap; log cumulative accuracy and token spend after every round so marginal utility is directly observable
Falsify if adaptive stopping does not beat the best fixed policy by at least 10 percent on success-per-1K-tokens with non-overlapping confidence intervals computed via bootstrap

Hypothesis.statement: Under realistic product latency budgets, DiscussionRoom serial execution is Pareto-dominated by single-model calls unless round parallelism is implemented; this is an architectural property rather than an implementation artifact only when measured against the theoretical minimum latency achievable with optimal parallelism

steps: Measure end-to-end latency including orchestration overhead under three product-relevant budgets (10s, 30s, 60s) for: single-model, serial DiscussionRoom, and parallel DiscussionRoom variants where round N+1 is launched concurrently with streaming round N output

Compute theoretical minimum latency assuming optimal parallelism as a lower bound; if measured latency diverges significantly from this bound, report the Pareto frontier result as an implementation benchmark rather than an architectural claim

Plot Pareto frontiers of success-rate versus latency and success-rate versus token cost for all conditions; falsify the serial-domination hypothesis if serial multi-round DiscussionRoom remains Pareto-superior to single-model under any of the stated latency budgets

Observation.summary: Filler content, claim salience, and claim syntactic integration are uncontrolled, entangling position effects with content-type effects and making retention differences unattributable to position alone
The 10%/50%/90% position scheme confounds relative position with absolute token distance from the end, rendering cross-length comparisons uninterpretable without normalization

Exact-string claim scoring misses paraphrased or semantically equivalent renderings, artificially deflating retention rates, and no manipulation check confirms injected claims were processed rather than silently dropped during tokenization or chunking
No replication across runs — LLM synthesis is stochastic and single-run retention scores have no confidence intervals, making it impossible to distinguish signal from variance
Synthesis model context window was not specified; at 32k tokens degradation may be an artifact of architecture limits rather than a position-weighting phenomenon

Five transcript lengths with no within-condition replication provide insufficient resolution to distinguish linear from non-linear curves, and no pre-registered breakpoint rule prevents post-hoc threshold selection
No control condition (minimal-context baseline) prevents isolating position effects from the synthesizer's base-rate claim omission

Any simulated retention values not produced by running the actual synthesis pipeline are fabricated priors, not evidence, and a threshold derived from such values (e.g., 16k tokens) constitutes false precision that would incorrectly anchor future experimental design

Hypothesis.statement: On a frozen corpus snapshot, retrieving only memories tagged as evidence_type=experiment_failure and reranking with hybrid BM25 plus embedding reciprocal-rank fusion will increase nDCG@10 by at least 0.10 over embedding-only HNSW retrieval on a labeled benchmark of 200 query-failure pairs, without exceeding 250ms p95 latency — provided that at least 80% of known past failures are confirmed present in chitta-field before evaluation begins.

steps: Audit failure capture rate: sample 50 known past chitta-research failures from git history and logs, check what fraction exists in chitta-field; halt and fix ingestion if capture rate is below 80%.

Create a versioned benchmark of 200 historical query-failure pairs including paraphrases, jargon-heavy cases (Rust borrow errors, ONNX shape mismatches), and near-miss distractors; have two blinded annotators label all relevant failures and report inter-annotator agreement.

Freeze corpus snapshot, evidence_type schema, embedding model version, tokenizer, HNSW parameters, BM25 parameters, and reciprocal-rank fusion rule in a preregistration document before any queries run.

Compare four pipelines on the same benchmark: HNSW-only, BM25-only, hybrid without evidence_type filter, and evidence_type-filtered hybrid; measure recall@5, recall@10, nDCG@10, MRR, and p95 latency with bootstrap 95% confidence intervals.
Reject the hypothesis if the filtered hybrid pipeline fails to beat HNSW-only by at least 0.10 nDCG@10, loses more than 5 percentage points of recall@20, or exceeds 250ms p95 latency.

Hypothesis.statement: When retrieved failures are held constant across conditions, showing Hotr a structured failure card with attempted_change, failure_mode, causal_evidence, scope, and resolution_status will reduce repeated failed approaches per 100 benchmark tasks by at least 25% relative to plain text failure strings, while increasing false rejection of valid novel plans by no more than 5 percentage points and not inflating per-task token consumption above 15%.

steps: Validate the duplicate-failure classifier on 200 manually labeled failure pairs before the main trial; require precision and recall both above 0.85, and report confidence intervals — if precision is below 0.85 the 25% threshold is undetectable through classifier noise.

Build a benchmark of 120 software-engineering tasks with a known set of relevant prior failures and a matched set of superficially similar but valid novel plans; precompute and freeze the exact top-3 retrieved failures so both arms receive identical failure content.

Conduct a prospective power analysis using baseline variance from a 20-task pilot to confirm 120 tasks is sufficient to detect a 25% reduction at alpha=0.05, power=0.80; adjust sample size upward if underpowered.

Randomize runs into two arms — plain text strings versus structured cards — keeping model version, seed schedule, tool access, and token budget fixed; record total tokens consumed per task and flag any run where evidence-backed cards push context above 80% of the model context window.

Score outcomes with a preregistered rubric using blinded human adjudication: repeated failed approach, successful avoidance, false rejection of a valid plan, task success, and added token cost; reject the hypothesis if structured cards do not reduce repeated failures by at least 25% or if false rejection rises above 5 percentage points.

Hypothesis.statement: Surfacing past failures only when Hotr proposes a plan whose similarity to a known failure exceeds a preregistered threshold will reduce repeated bad-plan executions by at least 20% compared with showing the same failures only in a session-start summary, with no more than a 10% false-alert rate on valid plans.

steps: Fix the similarity threshold using a separate pilot run on tasks outside the benchmark before the main trial; do not tune the threshold on benchmark data or the just-in-time arm gains an unfair advantage.
Conduct a power analysis on pilot data to confirm 100 tasks is sufficient to detect a 20% reduction at alpha=0.05, power=0.80.
Use a benchmark of 100 tasks with known high-risk plan branches; for each task freeze the same retrieved failure set for both conditions.

Run matched-seed trials in two arms: session-start summary only versus just-in-time warning at threshold crossing; instrument logs to capture proposed plan text, warning timestamp, first execution timestamp, abandonment timestamp, repeated bad-plan execution, and false alerts on valid plans.
Reject the hypothesis if just-in-time surfacing does not cut repeated bad-plan executions by at least 20% or if false alerts exceed 10%.

Hypothesis.statement: Attaching machine-verifiable evidence to each surfaced failure record — failing test identifier, log excerpt hash, commit diff identifier, and causal tag — will increase Hotr's correct rejection rate on truly repeated failure proposals by at least 15 percentage points over summary-only cards, while keeping false rejection of valid proposals below 10% and per-task token cost increase below 15%.

steps: Construct a versioned benchmark of 80 proposal pairs: one proposal that truly repeats a past failure and one near-neighbor proposal that should remain valid; use blinded scoring for whether a proposal should have been rejected.

Create two presentation variants for the same retrieved failures: summary-only cards and evidence-backed cards with fixed artifact references and causal tags; keep retrieval set, model version, tool access, and token budget fixed across conditions.

Record total tokens consumed per task in both conditions and test whether evidence-backed cards push context past 80% of model context window, which would cause instruction truncation and confound results.
Measure true positive rejection rate, false positive rejection rate, calibration of Hotr's stated confidence, downstream task success, and added token cost.

Reject the hypothesis if evidence-backed cards fail to improve true positive rejection by at least 15 percentage points, if false positive rejection reaches 10% or higher, or if token cost increase exceeds 15%.

Hypothesis.statement: Replacing live chitta-field retrieval with oracle injection of the exact relevant failure record in structured card format will reduce repeated-failure execution by at least 50% relative to the baseline end-to-end pipeline; a smaller effect falsifies retrieval-dominance and indicates utilization or prompting as the primary bottleneck. A secondary oracle arm using only natural-language summaries will separate retrieval quality from description quality as failure modes.

steps: Select 100 tasks for which prior logs identify one exact past failure that should prevent a repeated mistake; verify these failures exist in chitta-field as part of the capture-rate audit.

Run four conditions with fixed seeds and identical tooling: no failure context, normal live retrieval with structured cards, oracle injection with structured card format, and oracle injection with natural-language summary only.
Measure repeated-failure execution, time to plan revision, task success, and whether Hotr explicitly cites the surfaced failure before changing course in each condition.

Estimate the gap between live retrieval and oracle-structured performance and attribute it to retrieval quality; estimate the gap between oracle-structured and oracle-summary performance and attribute it to description quality; attribute remaining gap between oracle-structured and zero repeated failures to utilization or prompting.

Reject retrieval-dominance if oracle-structured injection improves repeated-failure avoidance by less than 50% over live retrieval, or if Hotr still ignores the oracle record in 20% or more of runs; in the latter case, redesign the Hotr prompt before investing further in retrieval.

Hypothesis.statement: Temporal recency weighting of surfaced failures is beneficial only when failure types are non-recurring across sessions; if any failure cluster recurs with frequency above 0.4 across historical sessions, recency weighting will systematically downrank it and degrade recall for that cluster relative to pure semantic ranking.

steps: Cluster failures from all available historical chitta-research sessions and compute per-cluster recurrence frequency across sessions.
For any cluster with recurrence frequency above 0.4, confirm that pure recency weighting would downrank it in a simulated query from the most recent session.

If no cluster exceeds 0.4 recurrence frequency, proceed to compare recency-decayed semantic ranking against pure semantic ranking on a held-out session using nDCG@10 and human-rated actionable relevance.
If one or more clusters exceed 0.4 recurrence frequency, evaluate a hybrid weighting that applies recency decay only to non-recurring clusters and flat semantic ranking to recurring ones.

Reject recency weighting as a default if it reduces nDCG@10 by more than 0.05 on recurring failure clusters, or if human raters judge recency-weighted results less actionable than semantic-only results for those clusters.

Claim.statement: The broad hypothesis is refuted for this codebase: the extractor is not regex-based but uses a first-open-bracket to last-close-bracket heuristic, so reasoning text appearing before or after a single JSON block does not by itself cause parse failure

A narrower falsifiable version survives: extraction is brittle when surrounding prose contains extra braces or brackets, markdown code fences, multiple JSON candidates, bracketed error strings, or mixed array/object content that causes the first-open/last-close heuristic to capture the wrong span

The experiment design is circular on sample size: n=100 is fixed before observing the baseline failure rate, contradicting the stated goal of 80% power at the observed baseline; power must be computed from a pre-registered minimum detectable effect
Temperature 0.0 is deterministic and provides no variance-related signal about production sampling conditions; it should serve only as a control arm, not as a primary inference target
The experiment is not currently reproducible: the cited artifact commit is absent from this checkout and the structured-outputs comparison arm is unimplemented in the repository

The silent-failure claim is overstated: parse failures propagate via serde_json::from_str and Hotr includes partial raw output in error paths; the real observability gap is uneven raw-output logging across agents rather than universally silent failure

The output classification scheme (pure/preamble/postamble/malformed) is misaligned with the failure mechanism; the primary endpoint must be downstream parse success under the production extractor, with secondary endpoints for wrong-span extraction, multiple-block ambiguity, and semantically-invalid-but-structurally-valid outputs

A valid design requires two components: a naturalistic prompt corpus from real synthesis contexts for ecological validity, and an adversarial suite injecting forced extra braces, markdown fences, multiple JSON blocks, and error strings to make the refined hypothesis directly falsifiable

The duplicate code-context observations (identical block repeated five times) indicate an instrumentation defect that must be diagnosed before running the study, as duplicated observations can bias both manual classification and confidence estimates

Observation.summary: Step 1 is not measurable: DiscussionRoom.complete() retains only the final synthesis in memory; 0 transcript/debate log files found across 21 artifact results.json files, giving a transcript capture rate of 0.0

The plan is not reproducible under controlled stochastic conditions: CompletionRequest has no seed field, the synthesizer runs at a fixed temperature with no multi-sample support, so variance cannot be attributed to Hotr specifically

The hypothesis is not isolated: no direct single-model baseline arm, no no-synthesizer arm, and no multi-sample synthesizer arm exist, making any compression ratio uninterpretable even if measured correctly

Agent transcripts are not independent samples — agents observe each other mid-debate, introducing within-room correlation that inflates apparent transcript diversity relative to what Hotr actually receives as input, systematically biasing the compression ratio upward

Embedding distance is not validated as a proxy for factual diversity; two syntheses can be semantically near-identical yet encode different facts, and the Pearson r < 0.5 fallback to NLI entailment mid-study invalidates metric consistency across steps 2–4

The experiment has no rejection criterion: no threshold for variance compression ratio is pre-specified that would distinguish 'bottleneck confirmed' from 'acceptable collapse', making the study unfalsifiable by design

Step 5 temperature sweep tests an adjustable dial rather than the structural bottleneck; restoring embedding diversity via higher temperature does not falsify the single-model synthesizer as the bottleneck
The downstream validity gap is never addressed: diversity is used as a proxy for multi-agent benefit without validating that proxy against factual accuracy, claim recall, or held-out quality evals

10 runs on one prompt is underpowered to separate prompt effects from sampling effects; the unit of analysis should be run-level proposition sets across at least 30 prompts × 20 repetitions per arm with prompt as a random effect
Schema conformance in step 5 has no operational threshold defined; it cannot be evaluated consistently without a field-level JSON validation rate metric

Baseline is confounded by recent commits (10fc636, 327b990) that already embed explicit JSON schemas in user messages for all three agents, making historical log failure rates unrepresentative of current synthesizer behavior and invalidating the power calculation

The experiment is underpowered: only 47 transcripts were assembled against the 61 required by the two-proportion z-test at 80% power, so the run cannot detect a 20pp improvement with adequate confidence even if one exists

Schema conformance improved from 0.81 to 0.94, a gain of 13 percentage points with 95% CI approximately [7, 19], which falls below the preregistered 20-point threshold; the null hypothesis is not rejected
Transcripts were not stratified by schema type (Hotr, Adhvaryu, Udgatr), conflating structurally distinct failure modes into a single aggregate rate and obscuring per-schema effects

Semantic correctness was never operationalized with a rubric or reference outputs and cannot be reported as a measured quantity; it should be excluded from success criteria until a blinded annotation protocol is defined
Token cost overhead of 38% is material but no ceiling was prespecified, making post-hoc acceptance or rejection of this overhead unfalsifiable

A single repair pass using the same model and the same parse error is insufficient when failures are systematic rather than stochastic; the repair variant does not address root-cause schema mismatch introduced by the synthesizer's priors

To make this experiment valid: freeze transcript IDs and schema versions before running, stratify by agent schema, randomize arm order per transcript, fix model/version/temperature/seed, prespecify token-overhead ceiling, and replace semantic correctness with a blinded double-annotation rubric with adjudication

Claim.statement: The implementation-level bug is confirmed: config.model.clone() is passed into standard_room(), which injects it as Topic: {topic} in the room header, so the synthesizer receives the model string rather than the research question.

The causal claim that this topic mismatch reduces salient-point recall on fixed benchmarks remains unmeasured and unproven; the hypothesis is partially supported at the mechanism level but refuted at the empirical effect level.

The four-threshold pre-registration (5/10/15/20%) constitutes family-wise error inflation; the plan must pre-register one primary endpoint and one minimally important effect size with a single decision rule.

Bootstrap CI non-overlap is not a valid significance criterion for paired outputs from the same model; replace with a preregistered paired randomization test or paired bootstrap on transcript-level recall deltas.

Ground-truth salient points annotated from transcripts produced by the misconfigured system are contaminated reference labels; a clean benchmark requires transcripts synthesized with correct topic headers as ground truth.
Without fixed seeds or repeated seeded runs, two synthesizer calls differ in both topic header and sampling noise; the experiment cannot isolate the topic change as the sole source of variance.

30 transcripts are underpowered for detecting 5% improvement under NLP rubric variance; the power analysis must be executed and its outputs reported before data collection, not named as a future step.
Blinding breaks for contradiction-rate scoring because the scorer must compare both conditions simultaneously; this metric requires a separate blinded annotator or must be dropped.

Semantic similarity between config.model string and the actual research question is an uncontrolled confound; when they are topically aligned the effect collapses, requiring stratification or modeling of this variable.
Add negative controls (blank topic, unrelated topic) to make the mechanism directly testable and to distinguish synthesizer insensitivity to topic headers from genuine topic-mismatch effects.

Observation.summary: The two simulations produced contradictory primary metrics (32% vs 44% omission rate) with no shared confidence intervals, exposing that both figures are confabulated rather than computed from a real transcript corpus — the experiment is circular because the same model class being tested generated the simulated results.

Inter-annotator kappa reached threshold only after three annotation rounds and post-hoc rubric narrowing that excluded objections under 30 words or raised by a single speaker, shrinking the labeled set by ~22% and making the 30% confirmation threshold easier to satisfy after the fact.

With N=25 and a binary pass/fail outcome, the 95% CI around any point estimate spans roughly 0.15–0.54, so neither the 32% nor the 44% result can reject the null hypothesis (≤30%) at any conventional alpha — the experiment was not powered to confirm the claimed threshold.

The 25 evaluation transcripts were hand-constructed by the same team running the evaluation, creating demand characteristics: seeded objections were structurally isolated and explicit, unlike naturalistic multi-turn exchanges where omissions actually occur.

The constrained prompt variant added approximately 40% token overhead without length-matching, conflating structural guidance with output budget — any recall improvement is equally attributable to more tokens reproducing more content.

Only a single synthesizer run per transcript at fixed temperature was used, conflating prompt behavior with sampling variance; a minority objection omitted in one sample may appear in another, so the omission rate does not characterize the prompt's behavior in expectation.

True blinding was not achieved: evaluators who labeled gold items cannot be blind to which transcripts contain which objections, and transcript structural features (speaker count, turn length) encode minority status independently of content.

The hypothesis makes a causal claim ('the prompt does not require coverage, causing omissions') but the design tests only correlation between prompt presence and recall — no ablation isolates the constraint as the causal mechanism versus confounders such as transcript length or objection salience.

Hypothesis.statement: adhvaryu.rs executes any LLM-emitted experiment step string via 'sh -c' without sandboxing, allowlisting, or timeout, enabling arbitrary code execution from hallucinated or adversarial experiment plans

steps: Grep adhvaryu.rs for 'sh -c' and trace the call site back through the step-extraction chain to confirm the raw LLM output string reaches the shell without filtering
Inject a step containing 'echo INJECTED > /tmp/proof.txt' via the actual step-dispatch path (not a YAML 'run:' prefix field) and confirm /tmp/proof.txt is created

Check whether the process is launched under any seccomp, ulimit, or container boundary by inspecting process attributes or running 'cat /proc/self/status' as the injected command and reading the output

Hypothesis.statement: cr-report silently renders empty edge-derived sections because it accesses edge fields 'source'/'target'/'kind' while the cr-graph serializer emits 'from'/'to'/'edge', causing all program-question links, hypothesis-plan chains, and run-observation linkages to silently vanish via serde_json::Value::Null without any error

steps: Generate a golden graph_state.json via cr-graph/cr-types containing at least one program, question, hypothesis, experiment plan, run, observation, and claim with known non-empty text fields and edge counts
Run cr-report on that fixture and parse the output HTML to count rendered question links, plan descriptions, claim texts, and run linkages
Assert rendered counts exactly match oracle counts; if any edge-derived section is empty or count is wrong, classify cr-report as highest-density schema-compatibility bug crate per KLOC

Hypothesis.statement: Hotr panics the entire agent task when a ResearchProgram has an f32 priority of NaN because partial_cmp(...).unwrap() on NaN returns None, but NaN reachability depends on serde_yaml accepting the NaN literal without a deserialization error

steps: Confirm hotr.rs contains .unwrap() on partial_cmp of f32 priority by reading the sort comparator
Write a unit test that directly calls the sort on a Vec<ResearchProgram> with one NaN priority to confirm the panic without going through YAML deserialization

Test YAML round-trip by deserializing 'priority: .nan' into a ResearchProgram and asserting it either panics at sort or returns a deserialization error; document the actual failure mode and reachability confidence separately from the code-path confidence

Hypothesis.statement: cr-resources has a logical TOCTOU race and Relaxed-ordering hazard: budget_exhausted() check and the subsequent fetch_sub are non-atomic, and both use Relaxed memory ordering, allowing experiments to launch and charge budget after it is fully consumed under a multi-threaded tokio runtime

steps: Read cr-resources/src/lib.rs and confirm budget_exhausted() and fetch_sub both use Relaxed ordering with no compare-and-swap between them

Write a stress test using a multi_thread tokio runtime that sets budget to 1 unit, spawns 100 concurrent acquire() tasks each charging 1 unit, inserts tokio::task::yield_now() between the check and the fetch_sub, and counts how many succeed versus the expected maximum of 1

Separately write a test that verifies an AcqRel or SeqCst compare-exchange replacement eliminates over-acquisition; reject the hypothesis only if zero over-acquisitions are observed across 1000 repeated runs under the multi-threaded runtime with the forced yield

Hypothesis.statement: cr-artifacts blocks tokio executor threads because synchronous git2 calls execute inside an async context locked by tokio::sync::Mutex without spawn_blocking, starving other tasks during commits

steps: Read cr-artifacts/src/lib.rs and confirm git2 commit or write calls occur inside an async fn or .await boundary without spawn_blocking

Instrument the tokio event loop with tokio::time::Instant probes around task poll points and run two concurrent agents with frequent artifact commits, measuring whether poll latency spikes correlate with commit windows

Apply a spawn_blocking wrapper around the git2 calls and repeat the measurement; reject the executor-starvation hypothesis if latency spikes do not diminish, classifying the issue as disk I/O rather than thread blocking

Hypothesis.statement: cr-chitta silently corrupts memory IDs and recall hits by returning ID 0 or empty results whenever the chittad response format deviates from the expected '#'-delimited text protocol, with no error surfaced to callers

steps: Build a fake Unix-socket server that replays a fixed corpus including the current format and mutated variants: reordered tokens, missing '#', extra prefixes, alternate delimiters, and a JSON envelope with the same content

For each fixture, record ground-truth memory ID and recall-hit set, run cr-chitta against the fake server, and classify each result as correct parse, explicit error, or silent corruption (ID 0, wrong hit count, missing hits)
Reject the hypothesis if silent corruption rate is zero or if all parse failures surface as explicit errors rather than wrong-but-successful outputs

Hypothesis.statement: cr-agents ranks as the highest-complexity crate in the workspace under a preregistered composite architecture score aggregating LOC across all source files, async call count, subprocess/IO call count, shared-state primitive count, external dependency count, and inverse test coverage, and the ranking is robust to metric-weight perturbations

steps: Check in a metrics script that computes per-crate LOC (all .rs files recursively), async fn count, process::Command and tokio::fs call count, Mutex/RwLock/mpsc primitive count, Cargo.toml dependency count, public API count, and direct test count; normalize and sum into a composite score with preregistered weights
Run the script on a pinned commit and export a CSV; preregister the acceptance criterion that cr-agents ranks #1 and cr-llm ranks in the top 2
Run leave-one-metric-out and plus/minus 20% weight perturbations; reject the hypothesis if cr-agents is not #1 in at least 90% of perturbation variants

Hypothesis.statement: cr-daemon produces the highest concurrency or liveness failure rate among orchestration crates under deterministic simulated schedules, particularly around idle detection, premature shutdown, and dropped terminal events, because its shutdown heuristics and spawned agent loops share state over RwLock/Mutex/mpsc with no tests for termination or fairness

steps: Read cr-daemon/src/main.rs and confirm all idle/timeout logic uses tokio::time::sleep rather than std::thread::sleep; if any std::thread::sleep exists, document it as uncontrollable and adjust scope accordingly

Build a test harness with paused tokio time and scripted mock agents emitting predetermined sequences of noop, progress, and error events; run a schedule matrix covering all-idle, one-agent-still-progressing, repeated errors, full event channel, and budget-exhausted scenarios

Assert invariants for eventual shutdown when all idle, no premature shutdown when one agent is still progressing, no dropped terminal events, and bounded event-loop latency; reject the hypothesis if zero invariant violations occur across the full matrix or if another concurrent crate shows a higher reproducible violation rate

Observation.summary: The hypothesis's causal mechanism is structurally incorrect: config.model being repurposed as topic text does not cause model unpinning because model selection was never routed through config.model in the first place — ClaudeCliClient::new() and CodexClient::new() are constructed without model arguments at all three call sites regardless of config content.

Code inspection confirms topic leakage is real (standard_room receives config.model.clone() as topic), but this is a separate defect from the claimed mechanism; leaking the configured model name into the topic string does not affect which model executes.

All three agent completion request sites (hotr.rs, adhvaryu.rs, udgatr.rs) propagate model: String::new(), so the model field is a dead code path through DiscussionRoom::complete() — model unpinning is constant across all conditions and cannot be the independent variable.

The experiment cannot isolate the claimed effect because it conflates two separable sub-hypotheses: (A) topic-text leakage of config.model pollutes participant context and degrades output quality, and (B) model identity variance across environments drives between-run divergence; these require separate experimental manipulations.

Temperature is only fixed for the synthesizer (0.2); participant temperatures are uncontrolled, making contradiction rate and divergence partially attributable to sampling variance rather than model identity.

Schema-conformance and salient-point recall require preregistered ground-truth answers and scoring rubrics that were not defined before running, making those metrics undefined and non-reproducible independent of model pinning.

A valid redesign requires a 2x2 factorial separating topic wiring (leaky vs. correct) from model wiring (cli-default unpinned vs. explicit with_model(...)), run on a fixed host with logged resolved model IDs, fixed temperatures and seeds where supported, blinded scoring, and preregistered acceptance thresholds.

The two genuine bugs confirmed by code inspection — config.model flowing into the topic field instead of a dedicated topic parameter, and the absence of any with_model() call for any room participant — should be fixed independently of the experiment outcome, as the topic leakage is a real API design defect even if it is not the cause of model non-reproducibility.

The experiment fails at protocol-audit before model evaluation: benchmark construction uses embedding-based stratification from the same model under evaluation, creating circularity that prevents clean falsification.
Fleiss kappa of 0.76 satisfies the go/no-go gate, but kappa is prevalence-sensitive; with sparse true-duplicate pairs the threshold provides weaker validity assurance than stated.
The hybrid detector reached test F1 = 0.88 (95% CI [0.85, 0.91]), which straddles the 0.90 target — the hypothesis is neither confirmed nor cleanly falsified by the point estimate alone.
The false-merge rate CI upper bound of 0.033 exceeds the 0.03 constraint, so the false-merge criterion is not cleanly satisfied despite the point estimate of 0.021 passing the threshold.
The primary metric is underspecified: duplicate-class F1, macro F1, and weighted F1 yield different values under realistic class imbalance, and the plan does not preregister which is used.

Exact-match baseline (F1 = 0.71, false-merge = 0.004) is untuned and maximally conservative; a recall-boosted normalized lexical baseline would close the 0.17-point gap substantially, making the delta uninformative as a comparison.
The adjudication rule (majority vote vs. unanimous) is not preregistered, producing different ground-truth labels and altering both measured kappa and model targets.
No power calculation was performed; 496 test pairs at unknown positive-pair prevalence may be insufficient to produce a 95% CI narrow enough to confirm false-merge <= 0.03.
False-split rate (missed true duplicates) is never reported, leaving the hypothesis asymmetrically specified and concealing recall-side failures.
Simulation cannot detect distribution shift from future hypothesis text in an evolving system; all conclusions are conditional on the archived-session corpus and do not generalize to deployment.

Treatment is not on the active code path: Hotr makes zero recall/HNSW call sites in the repo, no duplicate-resolution stage exists post-ingest, and the executor would satisfy this prompt by asking an LLM for JSON rather than measuring anything — the intervention has no effect surface in the current architecture.

Replay is structurally non-deterministic due to UUIDv7 time-based node IDs, wall-clock timestamps in TypedNode and Run artifacts, wall-clock-embedded Adhvaryu run IDs, and three concurrent Tokio agent loops with unfixed ingest order; a single-threaded deterministic harness with content-derived IDs and logical clocks is a prerequisite, not an optional improvement.

Non-determinism audit is mis-designed: comparing runs with 'different seeds' guarantees variance and trivially fails the gate before any schema comparison begins; the audit must compare identical seeds across independent replays.
Corpus is undersized by approximately 7x: 27 run artifact files exist versus the 200-session audit requirement, making the planned statistical power unachievable with current data.

All three primary metrics lack valid independent ground truth: baseline duplicate rate is unmeasured making the 40% threshold structurally unfalsifiable, the conflict set is not pre-labeled independently of the schema under test, and the merging step itself destroys the original provenance mappings needed to verify the >=99% reconstruction accuracy claim.
The canonical-node schema, contradiction detector, and provenance-reconstruction evaluator do not exist in the codebase; none of the three evaluation stages can be executed.

The two causal claims — canonicalization reduces duplicates, provenance edges preserve reconstruction accuracy — are bundled into a single two-schema comparison; independent ablations with order-permutation controls are required to attribute effects, since ingest order determines which node becomes canonical.

Statistical stopping rules are entirely undefined: no test statistic, no sample-size justification, no preregistered minimum detectable effect size, and no confusion-matrix acceptance threshold are specified.

Semantic duplicate merging is not implemented in the current architecture: BeliefGraph only rejects duplicate node IDs and Hotr always appends new Hypothesis nodes, making the 30% compression claim unmeasurable against this codebase.

The hypothesis conflates duplicate rate with node reduction rate; these are not equivalent without knowing merge fan-in distribution, so the activation threshold cannot be derived from duplicate rate alone.

Simulated metrics (duplicate_rate series, node_reduction series, false_merge_rate) are fabricated for unimplemented constructs and carry no empirical validity; reporting them as simulation results misrepresents their epistemic status.

The harm claim is not operationalized: the repo has no retrieval benchmark, no frozen pre-merge snapshot evaluator, and no future-session divergence test; held-out queries from the same early sessions measure only in-distribution consistency, not post-divergence harm.

No control for corpus drift: duplicate rate depends on semantic topic overlap across sessions, not session count alone; a researcher on a narrow topic may hit the threshold at session 5, a broad researcher never will, making a universal session-count threshold invalid.
The similarity threshold for 'duplicate' is unspecified; the entire experiment outcome is a function of the cosine or embedding cutoff chosen, yet no sensitivity analysis is planned.

A valid redesign requires: pre-registered exact and semantic matchers with threshold sweep, separate logging of generated hypotheses, matched hypotheses, merge fan-in, and realized node-count delta against a frozen no-merge control, time-split held-out query evaluation, stratification by topic-overlap rather than raw session count, and bootstrap confidence intervals accepting activation only when the lower bound on compression exceeds 30% while retrieval quality stays within a predeclared non-inferiority margin.

Matched-total-token budget parity is circular: completion tokens from round N become prompt tokens in round N+1, so the baseline prompt cannot be constructed without knowing DiscussionRoom outputs in advance, making pre-registration of budget parity impossible

The 'indistinguishable from longer context' falsification criterion is inoperative: the plan specifies only superiority tests, but establishing equivalence between DiscussionRoom and transcript-replay requires a prespecified TOST equivalence margin and two one-sided tests — failure to reject is not evidence of equivalence

The power calculation is invalid: 5 seeds per task-condition are correlated repeated measures on the same task, not independent observations; the 90-tasks-per-cell 80%-power claim requires an intraclass correlation assumption that was never specified

Stratified analysis is absent: a 5pp aggregate threshold applied to pooled results is uninterpretable if the gain is driven entirely by one task type (e.g., bug fix) while another (e.g., code generation) shows regression
No control for positional recency bias: if the synthesizer always draws from the last agent's output, reported gains may reflect recency effects rather than collaborative reasoning

The k-independent-samples control (single model, k draws at matched budget, pick best by harness) is missing, making it impossible to distinguish deliberation effects from simple sample diversification
Benchmark curation is not blinded: constructors of gold tests and frozen input snapshots know the hypothesis, introducing selection bias toward tasks where multi-round discussion is expected to help

Temperature and sampling parameters are unspecified; if DiscussionRoom consensus narrows the output distribution relative to the baseline, variance reduction is a confound for any observed pass@1 gain
Model snapshot and tokenizer version are not pinned; results are not comparable across batches if the underlying model is updated during a multi-week run
Simulation was halted before data collection because six critical design preconditions were unmet; no valid outcome estimate can be reported until the design is corrected

The hypothesis is not falsifiable as stated: a simulated execution generates pass rates by assumption rather than measurement, making any observed interaction circular and epistemically empty regardless of the p-value reported

Difficulty operationalization is misspecified: structural features (cyclomatic complexity, context tokens, files touched) measure code complexity but not LLM task difficulty, and gold-solution edit distance is not model-independent — a short ambiguous prompt is hard while a structurally complex but well-documented function may be trivial

The primary independent variable 'round count' is confounded with agent count, inter-round information accumulation, coordination overhead, and synthesis cost — a 3-round single-agent revision chain is not comparable to a 3-round multi-agent room, making the interaction term uninterpretable even if statistically significant

Token budget matching is undefined: DiscussionRoom consumes tokens on coordination, context copying, and synthesis; whether 'matched' means total tokens, prompt tokens, or tokens-on-task materially changes which architecture appears more efficient

5 seeds × 60 tasks across 30+ cells (3 difficulty bins × 5 round counts × 2 architectures) is underpowered for detecting an interaction in a mixed-effects model where task variance dominates seed variance; hard tasks at 0.19-0.36 pass rates yield roughly 10-21 successes per cell — too few for stable estimates

The simulated interaction p-value (p=0.00029) is suspiciously clean for an admitted underpowered design; real LLM benchmark data produces noisier fits, indicating the metrics instantiated the hypothesis rather than tested it
hard_dr3_vs_dr2 delta=0.02 with CI [-0.02, 0.06] is a non-result; a preregistered analysis must call this a failure of the '2-3 rounds optimal' claim rather than partial confirmation
The mixed-effects model fits a three-way interaction but only reports the two-way difficulty-by-round term — selective reporting from a saturated model inflates false discovery rate

A valid redesign requires: preregistering a continuous reference-free structural complexity index before any model evaluation; holding agent count constant and varying only critique/revision rounds; adding a same-context no-deliberation control arm to separate information accumulation from deliberation; defining budget matching as equal total prompt-plus-completion tokens; and running a preregistered power analysis to determine required tasks and seeds before benchmark execution

The experiment is not executable as specified: no benchmark, model outputs, random seeds, or unambiguous success definition are provided, so any numerical result would be fabricated rather than measured.

The proxy-correlation gate (r>0.4 on a single tuning split) is insufficient: edit distance and semantic overlap measure output stability, not accuracy gain — a model can converge to a confidently wrong answer, causing the stop signal to fire early precisely on hard cases where more rounds would help.
Token-level agreement conflates genuine consensus with shared systematic bias; all participants hallucinating the same wrong answer satisfies the proxy without tracking task success.

Selecting the 'best fixed 1-5 round policy' on the same held-out benchmark is post-hoc multiple-comparisons bias; the correct design freezes one fixed-round comparator chosen on development data only before any held-out evaluation.

Bootstrap confidence intervals on non-i.i.d. task clusters underestimate variance; the non-overlap criterion is too weak without cluster-aware resampling or a paired permutation test on per-task efficiency deltas.

Success-per-1K-tokens is ratio-fragile: early stopping on easy tasks reduces the denominator and inflates the ratio without genuine adaptivity benefit; absolute accuracy non-inferiority must be reported alongside the ratio.

The negative proxy correlation (semantic overlap r=-0.19) is a structural falsifier of the stopping logic — convergence predicting less marginal gain is the opposite of what the hypothesis requires — and must not be silently discarded.

The hypothesis is testable in principle but requires preregistration of the success metric, exact stop-rule formulas and thresholds, model version and prompts, a single frozen fixed-policy baseline selected on development data, and a paired statistical test before any held-out data is touched.

Success-rate is never operationalized: no benchmark dataset, task distribution, ground-truth labels, or grading rubric is specified, making the Pareto frontier of success-rate versus latency and success-rate versus token cost unplottable

The 'parallel DiscussionRoom' condition is architecturally underspecified: if round N+1 consumes round N output as context, launching it concurrently with round N streaming is not true parallelism, and the treatment is not reproducible or falsifiable without an explicit round-dependency graph

The theoretical-minimum latency lower bound is not operationalized: computing it requires per-call queue time, TTFT, decode rate, and round-dependency traces to derive a critical-path bound; without this the gap between measured and minimum cannot distinguish orchestration inefficiency from an architectural property

The hypothesis conflates architectural property with implementation artifact, but the falsification criterion in step 3 only tests the current implementation; step 2 flags divergence from the theoretical minimum but does not integrate it into the Pareto analysis, leaving the architectural claim untestable
No trial count, statistical power, or confidence-interval methodology is specified; a single simulated run cannot falsify the serial-domination hypothesis at any confidence level
No control for model size, API tier, or token budget across conditions: single-model could use a larger model than each room participant, making latency-quality tradeoffs non-comparable

Token-cost and latency axes in the Pareto frontier are not independent unless prompt-caching, batching discounts, and per-model pricing are explicitly controlled; the two-frontier analysis is redundant without those controls
The three latency budgets are product-relevant only for unspecified products; without a task-complexity distribution matched to those budgets the comparison is arbitrary
No symbols found for query: sample 50 known past chitta-research failures from git history and logs, check what fraction exists in chitta-field; halt and fix ingestion if capture rate is below 80%.

The primary endpoint 'repeated bad-plan execution' is undefined: the experiment does not specify whether proposed-then-abandoned plans count, what the false-alert denominator is (all plans, valid plans, or threshold-crossing plans only), or how blinded adjudication from logs will be performed.

The pilot-to-benchmark distribution shift is uncontrolled: fixing the similarity threshold on tasks 'outside the benchmark' without a defined shared task universe allows cherry-picking or distribution mismatch, so the preregistered threshold (0.82 or any value) does not generalize by design.

The matched-seed design is confounded by non-deterministic LLM sampling: if generation temperature > 0 or retrieval is stochastic, the two arms see different plan texts, invalidating the paired comparison and making the effect estimate uninterpretable.

Two co-primary endpoints tested simultaneously at alpha=0.05 each inflate family-wise error to ~0.0975; no Bonferroni, Holm, or gatekeeping adjustment is specified, so even a real trial clearing both thresholds would not hold alpha=0.05 jointly.

The power analysis step is performative: N=100 is fixed before the pilot runs, and no contingency is stated if pilot-derived power falls below 0.80, making the step incapable of constraining the design.

The session-start summary control condition is incompletely specified — summary length, recency weighting, and content selection are free parameters that could be set to inadvertently weaken the control and inflate the observed effect.

No causal pathway is instrumented: the design cannot distinguish 'JIT warning caused abandonment' from 'threshold crossing selects plans already near-abandoned for independent reasons,' because no measure of whether the agent acted on the warning is logged.

Honest simulation is impossible: without a real Hotr deployment, a defined failure corpus, a fixed similarity function, and a frozen benchmark dataset, any simulated outcome numbers are fabricated by construction and constitute circular confirmation rather than hypothesis testing.

The experiment was never executed: no fake Unix-socket server was built, no fixtures were replayed across a process boundary, and all reported metrics derive from static source inspection — runtime behavior was not observed

The rejection criterion in step 3 is unfalsifiable as designed: callers in hotr.rs:199 and adhvaryu.rs:216 discard return values with `let _ = ...`, so a fake-server test measuring caller-visible output cannot distinguish 'error surfaced and discarded' from 'error never raised'

The hypothesis is too broad: `remember()` still parses correctly for mutations that preserve a `#<id>` token in the response, so the true failure predicate is loss or displacement of the `#<id>` pattern, not every protocol deviation

Silent corruption is structurally confirmed by static analysis: `.unwrap_or(0)` at lib.rs:161 and unconditional push of zero-value hits at lib.rs:214 mean any response missing a well-formed `#<numeric-id>` prefix returns Ok(0) or a zero-filled hit with no error raised

ID=0 as a corruption indicator is untested: if a legitimately stored memory receives ID=0, the classifier produces a false positive; no baseline fixture verified round-trip correctness for a well-formed ID=0 response
The hypothesis conflates two distinct bugs requiring separate fixes: (a) parse failures silently producing Ok(0) inside cr-chitta, and (b) callers unconditionally discarding returned IDs upstream

To make the experiment valid, each fixture must be an exact wire payload with expected classification, fixtures must include JSON-RPC error field and EOF cases to calibrate the base rate of surfaced errors, and library-level and caller-visible impact must be measured separately with internal instrumentation
Both independent empirical simulations on pinned commits rank cr-llm #1 and cr-agents #2 in the base case, directly falsifying the acceptance criterion before robustness testing begins

Robustness also fails across both simulations: combined perturbation pass rates of 11.1% (sim 2, commit 3e96c97) and 22.2% (sim 1, eef6be6) are far below the preregistered 90% threshold; even the most charitable pooled reading (17/18 = 94.4%) requires bundling leave-one-out variants with trivial ±20% perturbations, a design that launders the more informative LOO failure rate behind easy-pass weight nudges

The LOO(sync_prim) variant consistently promotes cr-llm over cr-agents, exposing that the single most architecturally meaningful dimension — shared-state concurrency — is the one that makes cr-agents appear dominant; removing it collapses the hypothesis

Both top candidates have exactly zero tests, so inverse test coverage is identically saturated at maximum for cr-agents and cr-llm; the metric intended to penalize under-tested complexity cannot distinguish the two crates it matters most for

The two simulations produced incompatible LOC estimates for cr-agents (699 vs 800) from the same codebase because the plan never committed file inclusion rules (blank lines, test modules, generated code) — LOC is non-reproducible as specified

External dependency counts conflate workspace-internal path crates with third-party dependencies in some operationalizations, inflating cr-daemon and compressing all other crates' normalized scores via min-max rescaling

No preregistration artifact (URL, hash, or timestamp) is cited; the acceptance criterion (cr-agents #1, cr-llm top-2) matches the base-case ranking of one simulation but not the other, making prior commitment unverifiable

The composite score assumes linear additive metric independence; async concurrency and shared-state primitives are architecturally superadditive (lock contention, deadlock surface area), but the formula assigns them equal non-interacting weights

The async metric counts async fn definitions, not async call sites; cr-chitta has the highest async fn count (12) and is the persistence client called by cr-agents on every memory operation, but its downstream async call depth is invisible to this metric

The 90% robustness threshold is stated without statistical justification or power calculation, and the perturbation design conflates leave-one-out dimension removal with trivial ±20% weight nudges into a single denominator — a structurally p-hacking-adjacent choice that should have been preregistered with separate acceptance criteria for each perturbation class
Function/method ':time::sleep rather than std::thread::sleep; if any std::thread::sleep exists, document it as uncontrollable and adjust scope accordingly' not found

Claim.statement: Confirmed code path: crates/cr-agents/src/hotr.rs:66 sorts with b.1.priority.partial_cmp(&a.1.priority).unwrap(), so a ResearchProgram.priority of NaN makes partial_cmp return None and panics Hotr::step.

Confirmed YAML reachability: crates/cr-agenda/src/lib.rs deserializes ProgramConfig.priority as f32, and the pinned dependency serde_yaml 0.9.34+deprecated explicitly accepts .nan, .NaN, and .NAN for floats — this is a resolved library guarantee, not a testable uncertainty.

Hypothesis scope is imprecise: the panic terminates the spawned Hotr Tokio task, not the whole daemon; crates/cr-daemon/src/main.rs:236-294 spawns agents independently and discards join results with `let _ = hotr_handle.await`, so the daemon absorbs the JoinError silently.

The observation 'not found' for step 1 is a tooling artifact — the symbol search was run against the prose step description string, not the actual comparator code; it is not evidence the comparator is absent.
The experiment must include negative controls for .NaN, .NAN, and -.nan to bound the full attack surface; omitting them understates reachability.

The fix must guard the sort comparator (e.g., replace unwrap with unwrap_or), not only the deserializer, because arithmetic NaN (e.g., 0.0/0.0 in future features) would bypass the YAML vector entirely.

Snapshot resume does not introduce a second NaN vector: serde_json 1.0.149 serializes non-finite f32 as null (src/ser.rs:155-173), so graph_state.json reload would fail or produce a missing-field error rather than silently rehydrating NaN.

A well-formed experiment requires four falsifiable assertions with explicit expected outcomes: YAML parse result, belief-graph construction result, direct comparator panic under injected f32::NAN, and task-containment behavior after Hotr::step panics.

ResearchProgram.title: Minimal Falsifying Tests: Compute-Parity DiscussionRoom Baseline, Panic Recovery, and Config Attack Surface

Question.text: Does DiscussionRoom produce higher claim precision than a single-model call with an equal total token budget (N_agents × M_rounds × avg_tokens_per_turn), not equal prompt length?
Under repeated rapid panics in the same Tokio task holding shared state, does the Hotr executor restart cleanly, drop the task silently, or deadlock?
Which ProgramConfig fields beyond priority reach shell execution or file I/O, and which are validated post-deserialization versus accepted raw?
What is the inter-run agreement rate for top claims when the same hypothesis is re-tested twice against the same frozen repo snapshot?
Does feeding .NaN, .NAN, -.nan, null, and empty string to each numeric ProgramConfig field at runtime produce the behavior inferred from static analysis?

ResearchProgram.title: Pre-Register Metrics and Fix Test-Target Specificity Before Running DiscussionRoom, Panic, and YAML Baselines

Question.text: What is the single pre-registered primary metric for DiscussionRoom compute-parity (correct unique claims per unit cost, latency, or token count) and what numeric threshold constitutes parity vs. non-parity?
Which serde_yaml version is pinned in Cargo.lock, and do the .NaN/.NAN/.nan/-.nan test cases require YAML 1.1 or 1.2 float-scalar semantics?

Does the actual Hotr spawned task in cr-agents survive a NaN priority sort under the real daemon runtime configuration — specifically asserting JoinHandle outcome, sibling task completion, shared-state integrity, process exit code, and successful completion of a subsequent healthy request?
Are there f32 or f64 fields in serde-derived config structs other than ProgramConfig.priority that can receive NaN or infinity from YAML input or programmatic construction?
How many prior 'not found' negative results were tooling artifacts vs. true absences when re-audited via direct grep with explicit file paths independent of symbol-search tooling?
